description_long: This object is the core of an apple that has been mostly eaten, with only the central fibrous core and a small amount of flesh remaining. The surface is irregular and uneven, with visible bite marks and indentations along the sides. The top retains a reddish-yellow skin with a sunken area where the stem was once attached, while the rest of the surface is pale, creamy, and slightly browned from exposure. There are visible brownish spots and streaks, indicating oxidation and possible bruising. The overall shape is cylindrical but tapers and twists, with jagged edges and a hollowed-out center. The object appears organic, moist, and soft, with a slightly rough and fibrous texture.
description: A mostly eaten apple core with reddish-yellow skin on top, pale and browned fibrous flesh, and irregular bite marks.
description_view_0: Shows the apple core's side with prominent bite marks and a sunken top with reddish skin.
description_view_1: Displays a flatter side with more visible browning and a deep indentation in the flesh.
description_view_2: Highlights the apple core's hollowed center, reddish skin on top, and uneven, fibrous sides.
description_view_3: Reveals the opposite side with more exposed core, jagged edges, and less skin visible.
attribute_visible_damage_or_blemishes: browning, bite marks, and bruising visible
